name: coding-anywhere
description: Self-hosted remote-development stack: mosh + tmux + SSH reverse relay (or DDNS direct) so any mobile device can reach your home Mac with persistent sessions.

interface.longDescription: AI-guided setup for mosh + tmux + SSH reverse-relay (or DDNS direct). Lets you keep coding from any device, with sessions that survive network switches and app restarts. Covers two architectures: ECS Relay (works behind any NAT) and DDNS + IPv6 direct (lowest latency).
defaultPrompt: 帮我搭一套从 iPhone 连回家里 Mac 的远程开发环境
我想能在咖啡馆/高铁随时进入家中 Mac 的 tmux 会话
ECS Relay 和 DDNS 直连这两个方案我应该选哪个？
